subtopics.title: Paper 1: Essay Writing
common_misconceptions: Longer essays always score higher (quality over quantity)
Using big words impresses examiners (clarity is better)
Only one type of essay appears in the exam
subtopics.teaching_note: WAEC English Paper 1: Essay (100 marks, 2 hours). Section A: Essay writing (choose 1 from 5 topics, 450 words). Tips: 1) Plan for 5-10 minutes. 2) Write introduction, body (3-4 paragraphs), conclusion. 3) Use linking words. 4) Check spelling and grammar. 5) Count words. 6) Neat handwriting. Common topics: education, corruption, technology, environment, youth unemployment, health. Practice with past questions: 2015-2024.

subtopics.title: Comprehension and Summary Preparation
common_misconceptions: Copying from passage is acceptable (must paraphrase)
Summary can be in point form (must be continuous prose)
Word count doesn't matter (exceeding limits loses marks)
subtopics.teaching_note: Comprehension (60 marks): 2 passages with questions. Techniques: 1) Read questions first. 2) Skim passage. 3) Answer in complete sentences. 4) Use own words. Summary (40 marks): Condense passage to specified words (usually 150 words). Steps: 1) Read passage. 2) Identify main points (ignore examples, illustrations). 3) Write in continuous prose. 4) Count words. 5) Use connectives. Time management: Comprehension 45 mins, Summary 30 mins.
